candidates.stmt:
I grew up in Hackleton and went to local schools, so I know this area and the problems our villages face. I’ve always cared about our community. I’ve run youth groups and supported families through my work with the YMCA, helping young people get a better start in life. I also spent over 30 years working with charities overseas, helping to improve education and support for ordinary people in poorer countries.

Today, I work as a handyman here in Northamptonshire. I meet local people every day, fix problems, and listen to what matters to them. I know what it means to work hard and get things done. I’ve already taken action locally. I pushed the council to deal with traffic fumes and I speak up at Council meetings on subjects like local investment and asylum hotels.

It’s a privilege to be the Labour candidate in this by-election. I live locally on Grange Park and am on Grange Park Parish Council, so I know how to get things done. Previously I worked as a deputy director of education, and before that was a vicar. I’m married with three grown up daughters.
If elected I pledge to:
• Speak up for our villages at West Northants Council - it can be very Northampton centric.
• Make sure the Reform-led council makes good use of government funds to fix potholes.
• Champion our beautiful environment, nature diversity and combat fly-tipping.
• Campaign to end violence against women and girls.
• Hold regular surgeries to take up your concerns. I’ll always listen to you
